recalls.Description:
Wagner is recalling its 900 Series power steamers due to a significant burn hazard. The recall includes the models 905e Auto Steamer, 915e On-Demand Power Steamer, and 925e Steam Machine Elite Steamer. Each model features a yellow and black pressurized boiler base with the 'Wagner' logo, a clear accessory storage compartment, a handle, wheels, an eight-foot steam hose, and a nozzle with a trigger for steam control. The models may have their numbers displayed on the sides of the units.

The primary concern is that the hose can become excessively hot, and the nozzle can release hot water during and after use, posing a serious risk of burns to users. Consumers are urged to stop using these steamers immediately and reach out to Wagner for a free repair kit that includes a hose sleeve, nozzle cover, and funnel.
recalls.Title: Wagner 900 Series Power Steamers Recalled Due to Burn Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Wagner at 800 962-6118 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email us-wagnerrecall@wagner-group.com, or visit www.wagnerspraytech.com/900-series-recall. More information is also available by clicking on 'Product Recalls' on the Wagner website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Wagner Model 905e Auto Steamer, pictured with included accessories
Recalled Wagner Model 915e Power Steamer, pictured with included accessories
Recalled Wagner Model 925e Elite Steamer, pictured with included accessories

Injuries.Name: Wagner has received at least 156 incidents involving the hose getting too hot or the nozzle/gun expelling hot water. This includes over 50 reported burn injuries to consumers' arms, hands, feet, and face, many of which resulted in first- or second-degree burns.

Importers.Name: Wagner Spray Tech Corp. of Plymouth, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Burn hazard due to excessive heat from hose and hot water expulsion from nozzle

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit including hose sleeve, nozzle cover, and funnel

recalls.metaDescription: Wagner recalls 900 Series power steamers due to burn hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free repair kit.

recalls.Description: The recall affects the portable Dupray Neat Steam Cleaner, which is intended for cleaning, detailing, sanitizing, and deodorizing various household surfaces. This model features a white base with a black flexible hose and is identified by Model Number DUP020WNA. The affected batch codes range from 0118-01 to 0425-05, located on the rating label on the bottom of the unit. The cleaner is sold with an accessory set that includes brushes, tools, a Velcro pad, and dust cloths.
recalls.Title: Dupray USA Recalls Neat Steam Cleaners Due to Burn Hazard Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled steam cleaner and contact Dupray USA at 800 881-8482 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email recall@dupray.com, or visit www.dupray.com/recall. Alternatively, go to www.dupray.com and click on the "Neat™ Steam Cleaner Recall" banner for more information.

Products.Name: Dupray Neat Steam Cleaner
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 651,145 (In addition, about 96,530 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Dupray Neat Steam Cleaner
Recalled Dupray Neat Steam Cleaner accessory set
Recalled and Free Replacement Safety Boiler Cap
The Model Number and Batch/Date Numbers are located on the rating label, which can be found on the bottom of the cleaner

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of boiler ruptures, including four reports of minor burns, bruises, lacerations, and a broken wrist, and at least seven reports of minor property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at dupray.com, as well as in stores and online at Home Depot, Lowe's, Macy's, Walmart, Amazon.com, and other retailers across the nation from April 2018 through December 2025, priced around $150.

Importers.Name: Dupray USA LLC, of Newark, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Risk of burn hazards or serious injury from potential boiler rupture.

Remedies.Name: Consumers can obtain a free replacement safety boiler cap by visiting dupray.com, using their batch code found on the bottom of the cleaner to check eligibility. The new cap is easy to install with a simple screw-on mechanism.

recalls.metaDescription: Dupray USA recalls Neat Steam Cleaners due to burn hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement safety cap.

recalls.Description: Aterian is recalling the PurSteam Elite Travel Steamers and PurSteam Mighty Lil Steamers due to a significant risk of burns. This recall affects units purchased after December 2020 or those with specific date codes: 2310, 2308, 2305, 2304, 2303, 2212, 2211, 2210, 2203, 2112, 2111, 2110, 0221, 1019, and 4619. The PurSteam Elite model features a triangular steam vent opening, while the Mighty Lil model has an oblong opening, both with the PurSteam logo and model numbers on the bottom.
recalls.Title: Aterian Recalls PurSteam Elite and Mighty Lil Steamers Due to Burn Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Aterian at 833-910-6095 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@pursteam.com, or visit recall.pursteam.com. Additional details are available at https://pursteam.com/ by clicking on 'Product Recall.'

Products.Name: PurSteam Elite Travel Steamer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 75,400 PurSteam Elite Travel Steamers and about 119,000 PurSteam Mighty Lil Steamers

Images.Caption: Recalled PurSteam Elite Travel Steamer – Model PS-510
Label on bottom of recalled PurSteam Elite Travel Steamer – Model PS-510
Recalled PurSteam Mighty Lil Steamer – Model PS-550
Label on bottom of recalled PurSteam Mighty Lil Steamer – Model PS-550

Injuries.Name: Between December 2020 and 2024, there were 80 reports of hot water being expelled from the PurSteam Elite Travel Steamer model PS-510, including 14 reports of burn injuries. Between December 2020 and 2024, there were 392 reports of hot water being expelled from the PurSteam Mighty Lil Steamer model PS-550, including 40 reports of burn injuries, two of which were reported as second-degree burns. There are additional reports of hot water being expelled from both steamer models, including reports of burn injuries, from before Aterian's acquisition of PurSteam in December 2020.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Pursteam.com, Amazon.com, and Walmart.com from December 2020 to April 2025, priced between $15 and $35 for the PurSteam Elite model PS-510, and from December 2020 to January 2024, priced between $11 and $30 for the PurSteam Mighty Lil model PS-550.

Importers.Name: Aterian, Inc., of Summit, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of burns from hot water expulsion

Remedies.Name: Stop using the steamers immediately and submit a Recall Request at recall.pursteam.com. Consumers must provide their contact details, cut the steamer's cord, and upload a photo of the cut cord to receive a full refund. Keep the steamer until the refund is processed, as some may need to return it using a prepaid shipping label.

recalls.metaDescription: Aterian recalls PurSteam Elite and Mighty Lil Steamers due to burn risks. Learn how to get a refund and ensure your safety.

recalls.Description:
Northern Tool + Equipment has issued a recall for select NorthStar pressure washers due to a potential fire hazard. The recall affects three specific models: the NorthStar Gas Wet Steam and Hot Water Pressure Washer (item number 157310), the NorthStar Gas Hot Water Commercial Pressure Washer (item number 157594), and the NorthStar Trailer-Mounted Hot Water Commercial Pressure Washer (item number 157595). These models may have a MONTHYEAR code in the product serial number between 0125 (January 2025) and 0825 (August 2025), and a WEEKYEAR burner date code between 0125 and 2625, which indicates the weeks of 2025. The relevant codes can be found on labels located on the engine and various parts of the washers. 

Consumers are urged to stop using these pressure washers immediately and reach out to Northern Tool + Equipment for a free repair. The affected pressure washers can produce smoke or flames due to a failure in the fan wheel, creating a serious fire hazard. For more information, consumers can visit the Northern Tool website or contact their customer service.
recalls.Title: Northern Tool + Equipment Recalls NorthStar Hot Water Pressure Washers Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Northern Tool + Equipment at 888-518-0342 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, via email at inquiry@northerntool.com, or visit www.northerntool.com/recall-details-pw for further details.

Products.Name: NorthStar Gas Wet Steam and Hot Water Pressure Washer

Images.Caption: NorthStar Gas Wet Steam and Hot Water Pressure Washer (Item #157310)
NorthStar Gas Wet Steam and Hot Water Pressure Washer (Item #157310) - Serial number location on engine and burner date code location under battery box
NorthStar Gas Hot Water Commercial Pressure Washer Skid (Item # 157594)
NorthStar Gas Hot Water Commercial Pressure Washer Skid (Item # 157594) - Serial number location on engine and burner date code location under ball valve assembly
NorthStar Trailer-Mounted Hot Water Commercial Pressure Washer (Item #157595)
NorthStar Trailer-Mounted Hot Water Commercial Pressure Washer (Item #157595) - Serial number location on engine and burner date code location under NorthStar logo

Manufacturers.Name: Northern Tool + Equipment Manufacturing, of Faribault, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available at Northern Tool + Equipment stores nationwide and online at NorthernTool.com, Amazon.com, Ebay.com, and GNEDI.com from January 2025 to August 2025, priced between $5,000 and $10,000.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to potential fan wheel failure

Remedies.Name: Free repair by an authorized technician

recalls.metaDescription: Northern Tool recalls NorthStar Hot Water Pressure Washers due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free repair.

recalls.Description: TTI Outdoor Power Equipment has issued a recall for specific models of RYOBI brushless electric pressure washers, namely the RY142300 and RY142711VNM. These pressure washers are identifiable by their green color, a spray nozzle, a blue-cap detergent tank, and accessory storage. The model number and serial numbers can be located on the data plate on the side of the motor housing. The affected serial number ranges for each model are provided for consumer reference.
recalls.Title: RYOBI Pressure Washers Recalled by TTI Outdoor Power Equipment Due to Projectile Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach TTI Outdoor Power Equipment at 800-597-9624 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.ryobitools.com/recall for more details.

Products.Name: RYOBI 2300 PSI Electric Pressure Washer
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 764,000 (In addition, about 16,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Ryobi Electric Pressure Washer, model RY14230
Recalled Ryobi Electric Pressure Washer, model RY142711VNM
The model and serial number can be found on the data plate located on the side of the motor housing

Injuries.Name: TTI Outdoor Power Equipment has received 135 reports of capacitors overheating, including 41 reports of explosions, resulting in 32 injuries and/or fractures to the fingers, hands, face, and eyes.

Manufacturers.Name: TTI Outdoor Power Equipment, Inc. (TTIOPE), of Anderson, South Carolina

Retailers.Name: Available at Home Depot and Direct Tools Factory Outlet stores nationwide, as well as online at homedepot.com and directtoolsoutlet.com, from July 2017 through June 2024, priced between $300 and $400.

Hazards.Name: Capacitor overheating can lead to bursting, posing serious injury risks from ejected parts.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled units and check https://www.ryobitools.com/recall to see if their model is affected. A free repair kit, including a replacement capacitor and installation instructions, will be provided, with no proof of purchase required.

recalls.metaDescription: TTI recalls RYOBI pressure washers due to a serious projectile hazard. Learn how to check if your model is affected and obtain a free repair kit.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for the Aiper Seagull Pro cordless robotic pool vacuum cleaner, model number ZT6001. This unit weighs around 24 pounds and measures approximately 19.5 x 18.5 x 15 inches. The vacuum features the "Aiper" brand name prominently displayed on the front, with the model name and serial number, starting with S/N "61", located on the bottom label.
recalls.Title: Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Vacuum Cleaners Recalled for Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected pool vacuum immediately and reach out to Aiper at 888-968-0666, available from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email recall@aiper.com or visit https://aiper.com/seagullpro-recall/ for further details.

Products.Name: Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Vacuum Cleaner
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 32,660 (In addition, about 2,530 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Cleaner
Label with model name and serial number located on the bottom of the recalled pool vacuum

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 19 reports of the recalled pool vacuums melting, smoking or catching on fire while charging causing five incidents of property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Dongguan Yuanhong Intelligent Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, Home Depot, Lowe's, Walmart, and online at www.aiper.com, Amazon.com, Bestbuy.com, HomeDepot.com, Lowes.com, and Walmart.com from March 2023 to May 2024, priced between $700 and $900.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Aiper Intelligent Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Overheating adapter leading to burn and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Free replacement with Aiper Scuba S1 robotic pool cleaner and return shipping label

recalls.metaDescription: Aiper recalls Seagull Pro pool vacuum cleaners due to fire hazards. Stop using and contact Aiper for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Southern Telecom is recalling the Brookstone TurboVac Handheld Rechargeable Vacuums, identified by Model No. BSBVAC301. This model is marked on the back of the vacuum, while the brand name "Brookstone" appears on the front. The vacuums are designed in black, include a wall-mounting plate, and measure approximately 2.89 x 12 inches.
recalls.Title: Southern Telecom Issues Recall for Brookstone TurboVac Handheld Rechargeable Vacuums Due to Fire Risk; Available Exclusively at Belk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Southern Telecom at 866-450-4493 from 9 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email support@customersupport123.com, or visit www.southerntelecom.com and select "Safety Recalls" for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Brookstone TurboVac Handheld Rechargeable Vacuum (Model No. BSBVAC301)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of serious fires caused by the vacuums, resulting in property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available at Belk stores nationwide and online at www.belk.com from September 2022 through February 2024, priced around $90.

Importers.Name: Southern Telecom Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to potential short circuiting during or after battery charging.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled vacuums immediately and contact Southern Telecom for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Southern Telecom recalls Brookstone TurboVac Handheld Vacuums due to fire hazards. Contact for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: BISSELL is recalling its Multi Reach Hand and Floor Vacuum Cleaners, including models 1985, 19851 (also known as Multi Auto), 19859, 1985T, 2151, 21512, 21513, 21517, 21518, 21519, 2151A, 2151T, 2151W, and 2151V. These vacuum cleaners, identifiable by the 'Multi Reach' label on the hand vacuum, were available in various colors. The model numbers can be found on the rating label situated behind the dirt tank. It is crucial to properly dispose of the recalled lithium-ion batteries according to local regulations, as they require special handling and should not be discarded in regular trash or recycling bins.
recalls.Title: BISSELL Recalls Multi Reach Hand and Floor Vacuum Cleaners Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach BISSELL at 855-417-7001 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET Monday through Friday and 9 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET on Saturday. Alternatively, inquiries can be sent via email to RecallNA@bissell.com or through their website at www.BISSELL.com/recall. Additional information is available by visiting www.BISSELL.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' under the 'Support Resources' section at the bottom of the homepage.

Products.Name: BISSELL Multi Reach Hand and Floor Vacuum Cleaners
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 142,000 (In addition, about 14,600 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled BISSELL Multi Reach Hand and Floor Vacuum Cleaner with floor attachment
Recalled BISSELL Multi Reach Hand and Floor Vacuum Cleaner with handheld attachment
Location of the dirt tank
Product rating label located behind the dirt tank
A close up of the product rating label behind the dirt tank. The arrow is pointing to the model number

Injuries.Name: BISSELL has received 17 reports of the recalled vacuum cleaners smoking and emitting a burning odor. Six of the reports included the battery pack catching fire, three of which resulted in minor property damage and two resulting in minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's, Macy's, Kohl's, Target, Walmart, Best Buy, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at www.bissell.com, www.amazon.com, www.hsn.com, and other online platforms from August 2016 through December 2022, priced between $110 and $270.

Importers.Name: BISSELL Homecare Inc., of Grand Rapids, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and smoking battery pack, creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled vacuums and contact BISSELL for guidance on safely depleting the battery charge and obtaining a free replacement vacuum. Lithium-ion batteries should be disposed of per local laws, not in the trash.

recalls.metaDescription: BISSELL recalls Multi Reach Vacuum Cleaners due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: FNA Group has announced a recall for the Simpson Gas Pressure Washer model PS61264, which is primarily black in color. The unit features the Simpson logo, 'Pro Series,' and a 4400 PSI label on its handle, with the model number displayed on a silver label attached to the frame.
recalls.Title: FNA Group Issues Recall for Simpson Gas Pressure Washers Due to Burn Risk; Available Exclusively at Lowe's
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to FNA Group Inc. at 866-733-8468 from 11 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email voluntaryrecall@simpsoncleaning.com or visit https://simpsoncleaning.com/voluntaryrecall or www.simpsoncleaning.com and select the 'Voluntary Recall' tab for further details.

Products.Name: Simpson Gas Pressure Washer with Electric Start

Images.Caption: Recalled Simpson pressure washer (Model PS61264)
Recalled Simpson pressure washer (Model PS61264)
Recalled Simpson pressure washer Model PS61264 - front view
Recalled Simpson pressure washer Model PS61264 - top view
Recalled Simpson pressure washer Model PS61264 - side view
Recalled Simpson pressure washer Model PS61264 - side view

Injuries.Name: FNA has received two reports of batteries in the pressure washer's electric start feature overheating and damaging the units. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: FNA Group Inc., of Pleasant Prairie, Wisconsin

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's stores nationwide and online at lowes.com from March 2023 through July 2023, priced around $1,100.

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium battery in the electric start system presenting a burn hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled pressure washer and contact FNA Group Inc. to receive free repair instructions at an authorized facility. The repair will involve replacing the lithium battery with a sealed lead acid battery.

recalls.metaDescription: FNA Group recalls Simpson Gas Pressure Washers due to burn hazard. Contact FNA Group for repair instructions.

recalls.Description: Oreck has issued a recall for its Discover Upright Vacuum models UK30500 and UK30500PC. The affected vacuums have serial numbers starting with AJ2226 through AJ2232. These vacuums are identifiable by their silver and black design, featuring the 'Oreck' and 'Discover' branding on the front. The model and serial numbers can be found on a silver label located at the bottom of the vacuum.
recalls.Title: Recall of Oreck Discover Upright Vacuums Due to Shock Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Oreck at 888-383-7000 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, or visit www.oreck.com/pages/recall. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.oreck.com and click on 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the homepage for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Oreck Discover Upright Vacuum

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 41 reports of a prong detaching from its plug. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through independent Oreck dealers across the country and online at www.acmetools.com, www.amazon.com, www.homedepot.com, and www.oreck.com from September 2022 to September 2023, priced around $600.

Importers.Name: Royal Appliance Mfg. Co., d/b/a TTI Floor Care North America, of Charlotte, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Risk of electrical shock due to the vacuum's plug prong detaching and remaining in an outlet when unplugged.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled vacuums and contact Oreck or their local dealer for free repair instructions. To find the nearest authorized dealer, visit 'Find Your Local Store' on www.oreck.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Oreck recalls Discover Upright Vacuums due to a potential electrical shock hazard. Learn how to get a free repair.
